back: The process of sending data from one device to another.
Data is sent one bit at a time over a single wire.
Multiple bits are sent at the same time over multiple wires.

front: Give one advantage of parallel transmission
back: Faster over short distances.

front: Give one disadvantage of parallel transmission
back: Crosstalk or skew.

front: What is crosstalk in parallel transmission?
back: Interference between signals in adjacent wires, causing data corruption.

front: What is skew in parallel transmission?
back: When bits arrive at different times due to different wire lengths or delays.

front: Give one advantage of serial transmission
back: Reliable over long distances.
Data flows in one direction only.
Data flows both ways but not at the same time.
Data flows both ways at the same time.
A standard interface that uses serial transmission to connect devices.
Universal Serial Bus.
A type of connection used to link devices such as keyboards, mice and storage devices to a computer.
Provides power and data through one cable.
Plug and play (automatically recognised).
Devices can be connected or removed without restarting the computer.
Supports many different devices.
Limited cable length.
Can be slower than some other connection types.
